subject: ap-precalculus
title: Logarithmic Regression: Soil Temperature & Depth
stimulus:
A geologist measures soil temperature (in degrees Celsius) at various depths below the surface and records the following data:

| Depth $d$ (meters) | Temperature $T$ ($^\circ$C) |
|---|---|
| 1 | 12.0 |
| 4 | 18.4 |
| 9 | 22.6 |
| 16 | 26.0 |
| 25 | 28.7 |

The geologist proposes the model $T(d) = a + b\ln(d)$ to represent soil temperature as a function of depth.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the data at $d = 1$ and $d = 25$ to write two equations that can be used to find the values of constants $a$ and $b$.

(ii) Find the values of $a$ and $b$ as decimal approximations. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Two correct equations — $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0$, which simplifies to $a = 12.0$; and $a + b\ln(25) = 28.7$; equivalently, $12 + b\ln(25) = 28.7$

1 pt: Correct values $a = 12.0$ and $b = \frac{28.7 - 12.0}{\ln(25)} = \frac{16.7}{3.2189} \approx 5.189$ (accept values in $[5.18, 5.20]$); supporting work must show the substitution and division step
parts.scoring_notes: The first equation immediately gives $a = 12$ since $\ln(1) = 0$. The second equation then gives $b = 16.7 / \ln(25) \approx 5.189$. Decimal must be correct to three decimal places. Full credit for both values if shown correctly. Accept $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ as a complete response.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to predict the soil temperature at a depth of $d = 50$ meters. Express your answer as a decimal approximation.

(ii) A geologist at another site obtains data that, on a semi-log plot of $T$ versus $\ln(d)$, appears approximately linear with a positive slope. What does this indicate about the relationship between temperature and depth at that site? Explain your reasoning.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct prediction — $T(50) = 12 + 5.189\ln(50) = 12 + 5.189 \cdot 3.912 = 12 + 20.299 \approx 32.299$ degrees Celsius (accept values in $[32.2, 32.4]$); supporting work must show substitution

1 pt: Correct reasoning — a linear semi-log plot of $T$ vs. $\ln(d)$ indicates a logarithmic relationship $T = a + b\ln(d)$; a positive slope means $b > 0$, so temperature increases as depth increases; the plot being linear confirms the logarithmic model fits well
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $\ln(50) \approx 3.9120$, so $5.189 \times 3.9120 \approx 20.30$, giving $T \approx 32.30$°C. For part (ii), the key insight is that linearity on the semi-log ($T$ vs. $\ln d$) plot means the logarithmic model is appropriate, and positive slope means temperature increases with depth. A response that only says 'temperature increases with depth' without explaining the semi-log relationship earns only partial credit.

parts.prompt:
(i) Determine the depth $d$ at which the model predicts a soil temperature of $30$ degrees Celsius. Express your answer as a decimal approximation. Show the computations that lead to your answer.

(ii) The geologist observes that the actual temperature at $d = 36$ meters is $27.5^\circ$C, while the model predicts $T(36) = 12 + 5.189\ln(36) \approx 30.7^\circ$C. Explain what this residual indicates about the model's fit.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct depth — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) = 30 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) = 18 \Rightarrow \ln(d) = \frac{18}{5.189} \approx 3.469 \Rightarrow d = e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ meters (accept values in $[31.8, 32.4]$); supporting work must show the logarithmic solving step

1 pt: Correct interpretation — the residual is $27.5 - 30.7 = -3.2^\circ$C (actual minus predicted); the negative residual means the model overestimates the temperature at $d = 36$ m; this suggests the model may not fit perfectly at all depths, or the temperature-depth relationship changes at greater depths
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $18 / 5.189 \approx 3.469$, then $e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ m. For part (ii), the residual = actual $-$ predicted $= 27.5 - 30.7 = -3.2$. The interpretation must include: (a) the direction (model overestimates), and (b) what this means for the model's fit. A response that only computes the residual without interpretation does not earn the point.
